parallel--d05577ca-cd3e-4cfb-ae51-ba8854.name: Correlate successful external logins following failed attempts with indicators of compromise:

description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1133 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0354#AN1004 . The description of the approach follows: Unusual or unauthorized external remote access attempts (e.g., RDP, VPN, Citrix) → repeated failed logins followed by a successful session from uncommon geolocations or outside business hours → subsequent internal lateral movement or data exfiltration activities.

external_references.name: MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1133: External Remote Services

items.content: Deploy and configure monitoring tools to capture external remote access attempts (RDP, VPN, Citrix) on Windows endpoints and network perimeter devices.
Establish baselines for normal remote access patterns, including typical source geolocations, business hours, and user accounts involved.
Implement alerting for repeated failed login attempts from external sources, with thresholds adjusted to minimize false positives.
Check if the successful session originates from an uncommon geolocation (e.g., not in the allowed countries list).
Verify if the login occurs outside of established business hours for the organization.
Cross-reference the user account with known compromised credentials or suspicious activity reports.
Correlate successful external logins following failed attempts with indicators of compromise:
Immediately isolate the affected endpoint or user session from the network to prevent lateral movement.
Review authentication logs (e.g., Windows Event Logs for RDP, VPN provider logs) for additional context.
Scan the endpoint for signs of malware, persistence mechanisms, or unauthorized tools.
Check for subsequent internal lateral movement attempts (e.g., SMB connections, WMI executions, PsExec usage) from the compromised account/host.
Monitor for data exfiltration activities (e.g., large outbound transfers, connections to known malicious IPs).
Upon detection of a suspicious successful external login, initiate investigation and response:
Escalate to incident response team if lateral movement or data exfiltration is confirmed, and initiate full containment procedures.
Update detection rules and access controls based on findings to prevent recurrence.
